name: a health state and a general state on one entity must not cross the firewall
description: Red-team C1 (health flavour): a general-domain state fact and a health-domain state fact that share entity+predicate+qualifier must NOT form one supersession chain — candidate retrieval must be scoped to same entity+domain+kind (docs/ANALYSIS.md). Here Me.careStatus is asserted once in `general` ('living independently') and once in `health` ('admitted to rehab facility'). The correct graph keeps BOTH active, one per domain, with no fact_conflict. Previously pipeline._existing_facts read across domains, so the health fact superseded the general one and a cross-domain review leaked the other domain's text; fixed by domain-scoping the candidate read.

steps.body: Doing great — living independently in my own apartment.
Admitted to the inpatient rehab facility after the procedure.
